vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/auto-start
vBRIEFInfo.description: Auto-synthesized minimal plan for PAN-1775: feat(dashboard): remote (fly.io) work agents need a real session row in the issue tree — chip-only visibility reads as 'no agent'

plan.title: feat(dashboard): remote (fly.io) work agents need a real session row in the issue tree — chip-only visibility reads as 'no agent'
plan.author: panopticon-cli/auto-start

narratives.Problem:
## Problem

During the PAN-1762 remote Fable 5 test, the operator asked: *"why can't I see the work agent in the issue tree?"* — while the agent was running fine on Fly.

The remote agent IS surfaced, but only as a **tiny icon chip** in the issue row's resource strip (tooltip: `fly.io: pan-pan-1762-ws (running)`). Compare the two adjacent issues in the tree today:

- **PAN-1765 (local agent):** full session row — `Work (gpt-5.5) · 9s ago · working · 20m` with live last-heard, current tool, and an openable terminal.
- **PAN-1762 (remote agent):** no session row at all. Worse, the issue's headline badge was hijacked by the leftover idle planning session — it read `1 session idle · ! INPUT` while a work agent was actively implementing on Fly.

## Why

`feature.sessions` is built from local tmux discovery; remote agents have no local tmux session, so they never enter the sessions list. The PAN-1676 visibility pass added the chip + remoteAgent details (FeatureItem.tsx rows / resource strip) but not session-row parity.

## Fix

1. Synthesize a session entry from `remote-state.json` (`~/.panopticon/agents/agent-<id>/remote-state.json`) wherever feature.sessions is composed, labeled e.g. `Work (claude-fable-5) · fly.io · running`, so the issue header badge counts it as a running work agent (`1 work agent running`, not `1 session idle`).
2. Clicking it should open the remote terminal (tmux on the VM via `pan workspace ssh` / a ws-terminal remote bridge — or at minimum show remote agent output via `getRemoteAgentOutput`).
3. The idle planning session should not outrank an actively-working remote agent for the issue's headline status.

## Refs
PAN-1676 (remaining scope: "Dashboard visibility / overflow-slot surfacing for remote agents"), PAN-1762 (the live test that surfaced this).
narratives.Proposal: Implement the issue directly from the tracker-provided title and body.
